sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 42-year-old male, presented with a 2-hour history of low back pain that started after raking leaves in his yard.
He reports that the pain is worse when standing and improves with hot shower and sitting.
He denies any previous similar episodes, except for one 3-year-old episode that resolved within a day.
He tried ice and acetaminophen, but it did not help.
His partner reports that he also experiences pain when standing up, but not when bending over.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in his right leg.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient reports pain with flexion and palpation around the L5 area.
He has normal dorsiflexion and equal pulses in all extremities.
Test Results: The patient had a previous x-ray of his low back, which showed normal results.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a probable acute low back sprain.
Problem list: Acute low back sprain; Suspected radiculopathy; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5mg PO BID for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretching and exercises to improve mobility and strength.
I instructed the patient to rest and follow up with me in 2 weeks if the symptoms do not improve.
I will consider ordering an MRI if the symptoms persist.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in 2 weeks to reassess the condition and consider further testing, including an MRI if necessary.
